page.title: Mangusta 80 vs Ferretti 94: Top Luxury Yachts in Marbella

data.meta_description: Compare the Mangusta 80 vs Ferretti 94 in Marbella: 24m vs 29m, jet ski inclusions, capacity, and pricing. Book your luxury charter today.
data.summary: A side-by-side comparison of the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 for luxury yacht charters in Marbella, covering size, capacity, inclusions, and pricing.
key_facts: Mangusta 80: 24m length, 12 guests, departs Puerto Banús.
Ferretti 94: 29m length, 12 guests, departs Puerto Banús.
Mangusta 80 charter: €4,719 minimum 4h (jet ski included free).
Ferretti 94: pricing on request via WhatsApp.
Both yachts include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA.
Mangusta 80 available in grey and white variants.
Ferretti 94 offers superior deck space and interior volume.

faq.q: What are the main differences between the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 is 24m long and accommodates 12 guests, while the Ferretti 94 is 29m with the same capacity. The Mangusta 80 has a fixed 4-hour minimum charter at €4,719 with a free jet ski, whereas the Ferretti 94’s pricing is bespoke and on request.

faq.q: Which yacht is better for a day charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Both are excellent. The Mangusta 80 is ideal if you want a shorter, all-inclusive trip with a jet ski. The Ferretti 94 suits those seeking a larger, more spacious yacht for extended cruising or entertaining, though its pricing is custom.

faq.q: How many guests can each yacht hold?
faq.a: Both the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 are licensed for a maximum of 12 guests, making them perfect for group charters, celebrations, or corporate events.

faq.q: What is included in the Mangusta 80 charter price?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 charter at €4,719 for 4 hours includes a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, water, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, Spanish IVA (21%), and a free jet ski.

faq.q: How do I book the Ferretti 94?
faq.a: The Ferretti 94’s pricing is on request. Contact us via WhatsApp for a personalised quote based on your preferred duration, itinerary, and any extras. All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA.

faq.q: Where do these yachts depart from?
faq.a: Both the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s iconic luxury marina, giving you direct access to the Costa del Sol’s best coastal spots.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food and drink. The standard charter already includes a selection of drinks and light snacks, but you can cater further via WhatsApp arrangements.

faq.q: Which yacht has more deck space for sunbathing?
faq.a: The Ferretti 94, at 29m, offers significantly more deck space, including large sun pads and a flybridge. The Mangusta 80 is sportier and more compact but still has ample lounging areas for 12 guests.

data.body_html:
When comparing the Mangusta 80 vs Ferretti 94 for a luxury yacht charter in Marbella, you are choosing between two exceptional vessels that define opulence on the Costa del Sol. Both depart from Puerto Banús and accommodate up to 12 guests, but they differ in size, pricing, and onboard experience. This guide breaks down the key differences to help you decide which yacht best suits your day on the Mediterranean.

Size and Capacity: 24m vs 29m

The Mangusta 80 measures 24 metres in length, while the Ferretti 94 stretches to 29 metres. Both yachts carry a maximum of 12 passengers, but the Ferretti’s extra five metres translate into larger decks, a more spacious saloon, and additional interior volume. The Mangusta 80, built by Overmarine, is known for its sporty lines and performance, whereas the Ferretti 94 emphasises comfort and entertaining space. If you prioritise outright space for a group, the Ferretti 94 has the edge.

Pricing and Inclusions

The Mangusta 80 has a transparent starting rate: €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours, which includes a free jet ski, licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, water, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The Ferretti 94, by contrast, is pricing on request via WhatsApp, allowing for a customised itinerary and duration. Both charters include the same standard inclusions: skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and taxes. For a fixed-price option with a jet ski, the Mangusta 80 is the clear choice.

Onboard Experience and Features

The Mangusta 80 (available in grey and white variants) offers a sleek, open-plan layout with a large aft deck, sun pads, and a spacious flybridge. Its jet ski inclusion makes it ideal for water sports enthusiasts. The Ferretti 94 provides a more expansive flybridge, a formal dining area, and a full-beam master suite below deck, though as a day charter you will mostly use the exterior spaces. Both yachts are equipped with premium sound systems and modern amenities, but the Ferretti 94’s size allows for greater separation between lounging and dining areas.

Itinerary and Destinations

Departing from Puerto Banús, both yachts can cruise to Marbella’s top spots: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, Cabopino, or further west to Estepona and Sotogrande. The Mangusta 80’s sportier hull makes it quicker between anchorages, while the Ferretti 94’s displacement offers a more stable, leisurely ride. With a jet ski on the Mangusta, you can explore hidden coves more actively. The Ferretti 94, with its larger deck, is perfect for lounging and entertaining at anchor.

Which Yacht Should You Choose?

Choose the Mangusta 80 if you want a fixed-price charter under €5,000 with a jet ski included, and you value a sporty, performance-oriented yacht. Opt for the Ferretti 94 if you desire maximum deck space, a more luxurious interior, and are flexible on budget—contact us for a tailored quote. Both yachts deliver an unforgettable day on the water from Marbella’s most prestigious marina. For more options, explore our full fleet of luxury yacht charters in Marbella.

Frequently asked questions

What are the main differences between the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94?

The Mangusta 80 is 24m long and accommodates 12 guests, while the Ferretti 94 is 29m with the same capacity. The Mangusta 80 has a fixed 4-hour minimum charter at €4,719 with a free jet ski, whereas the Ferretti 94’s pricing is bespoke and on request.

Which yacht is better for a day charter in Marbella?

Both are excellent. The Mangusta 80 is ideal if you want a shorter, all-inclusive trip with a jet ski. The Ferretti 94 suits those seeking a larger, more spacious yacht for extended cruising or entertaining, though its pricing is custom.

How many guests can each yacht hold?

Both the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 are licensed for a maximum of 12 guests, making them perfect for group charters, celebrations, or corporate events.

What is included in the Mangusta 80 charter price?

The Mangusta 80 charter at €4,719 for 4 hours includes a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, water, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, Spanish IVA (21%), and a free jet ski.

How do I book the Ferretti 94?

The Ferretti 94’s pricing is on request. Contact us via WhatsApp for a personalised quote based on your preferred duration, itinerary, and any extras. All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA.

Where do these yachts depart from?

Both the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s iconic luxury marina, giving you direct access to the Costa del Sol’s best coastal spots.

Can I bring my own food and drink on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food and drink. The standard charter already includes a selection of drinks and light snacks, but you can cater further via WhatsApp arrangements.

Which yacht has more deck space for sunbathing?

The Ferretti 94, at 29m, offers significantly more deck space, including large sun pads and a flybridge. The Mangusta 80 is sportier and more compact but still has ample lounging areas for 12 guests.
